meta_title: For Students: client workflow to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Client Workflow to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Bridge the experience gap. Learn to master professional client workflows using Antigravity and Claude Code to build a high-impact portfolio today.
content.answer_block: To overcome the no-portfolio trap, simulate a high-stakes client project using a structured workflow. By integrating Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative development, you create production-grade features that demonstrate real-world engineering competence. This approach transforms abstract learning into a tangible, professional case study for recruiters.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a GitHub profile is a rite of passage, but it is also the greatest barrier to your career. You have the skills, but without a proven delivery track record, you are invisible to hiring managers who value process over syntax. Most students fail because they build toys, not systems.

Today, we shift your identity from 'student' to 'systems architect.' By adopting a professional client workflow—where requirements are treated as contracts and code as a business asset—you will turn a simple project into a robust case study. We are using Antigravity to architect your foundation and Claude Code to execute with the precision of a senior dev.

steps.title: Define the Client Requirements Document
steps.description: Treat your project like a paid contract. Define the scope, user personas, and technical constraints before writing a single line of code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Lead Technical Architect. Help me draft a professional Project Requirements Document (PRD) for a SaaS tool that solves [Insert Problem]. Include user stories, tech stack, and key deliverables.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review this PRD and suggest 3 technical edge cases I should account for in the architecture.'

steps.title: Scaffold the Infrastructure with Antigravity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to bootstrap a production-ready repository structure that mirrors enterprise standards, ensuring your project looks professional from the first commit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a clean, modular repository structure for a [Insert Stack] application. Include folders for services, models, controllers, and a professional README structure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize the project structure based on the generated architecture and create a git repository.'

steps.title: Iterative Feature Development
steps.description: Implement features in small, testable chunks. This shows you understand how to manage technical debt and maintain velocity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a task list for the first sprint of this project. Prioritize core functionality that demonstrates high-impact logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Implement the authentication module using best practices for security and error handling.'

steps.title: Automated Testing & Quality Assurance
steps.description: A portfolio project without tests is just a script. Integrate automated tests to prove you care about reliability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write unit tests for the core service layer. Ensure high coverage for edge cases involving database failures.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Execute the test suite and fix any regressions found in the last module.'

steps.title: Documenting the 'Why' for Recruiters
steps.description: Document your decision-making process in the README. Explain the trade-offs you made to show senior-level thinking.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a professional case study section for my README. Focus on the 'Why' behind my architectural choices and how I handled a specific technical challenge.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Update the README.md with the generated case study section and project status.'

pro_tips: Always commit with semantic message conventions (e.g., feat:, fix:, docs:) to demonstrate professional habits.
Include a 'Trade-offs' section in your README to show you understand that all engineering is a balance of constraints.
Use Claude Code to generate documentation for your API endpoints automatically; it proves you prioritize maintainability.
Focus on one 'hero' feature that handles complex data, rather than building ten simple CRUD pages.
Add a 'Future Scope' section in your docs to show you think beyond the current MVP.
content.next_steps: Deploy your project to Vercel or Railway, add a live demo link to your README, and share your case study on LinkedIn tagged with the technologies you used.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are a builder with a process. The industry is waiting for developers who can deliver, not just dream. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
